title: Conflict Stages, Semantic Resolution, and Rerere · Repository Repair Bay
summary: A responder inherits a plausible but defective three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier; preserve reachable history, isolate the first causal Git-state mismatch, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, stage 3 theirs, unmerged entries, and higher-order conflict metadata before editing.
Distinguish content, add/add, delete/modify, rename, directory/file, binary, mode, submodule, and recursive conflicts and select evidence for each.
Reconstruct intent from base and both histories, produce a semantic combined result, and test invariants that neither side tested alone.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Gita Conflict Regression Handoff evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Gita Conflict Regression Handoff assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Case G61 is a debug in a disposable repository. the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict. The learner must predict stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction, run git ls-files --unmerged, explain all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context, test this changed condition: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer. Competency probe: Record conflict cause, chosen invariant, rerere provenance, changed-case tests, and abort or continuation evidence for future integrators.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Gita Conflict Regression Handoff has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Gita Conflict Regression Handoff precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G61 is a debug in a disposable repository. the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict. The learner must predict stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction, run git ls-files --unmerged, explain all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context, test this changed condition: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer. Competency probe: Record conflict cause, chosen invariant, rerere provenance, changed-case tests, and abort or continuation evidence for future integrators.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Once rerere applies cleanly, human and behavioral review are redundant.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph for the debug Gita Conflict Regression Handoff case
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology for the debug Gita Conflict Regression Handoff case
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation for the debug Gita Conflict Regression Handoff case
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path for the debug Gita Conflict Regression Handoff case

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-conflict-regression-handoff-1pua3v" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Conflict Regression Handoff into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-conflict-regression-handoff-1pua3v
# scenario-key: trace-1pua3v probe-v3aup1 boundary-ua3v1p owner-1pua3vx fault-1pua3vz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1p candidate=b00b13v
# hypothesis: the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict
git ls-files --unmerged
# expected: stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction
# observation: all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context
# changed-case: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail
# after-ref: main=a11ce1p candidate=c0ffeev
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer
# transfer-limit: rerere output remains a proposal and must pass semantic changed-case verification
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Gita Conflict Regression Handoff structural check is only one gate. three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Gita Conflict Regression Handoff: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G69 is a debug in a disposable repository. the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict. The learner must predict stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction, run git ls-files --unmerged, explain all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context, test this changed condition: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer. steps.why: Retrieving Gita Conflict Regression Handoff through the concrete three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record conflict cause, chosen invariant, rerere provenance, changed-case tests, and abort or continuation evidence for future integrators.
